events.label: reasoning
events.text:
We are asked to assess an S-trap configuration in a commercial project at rough-complete stage. The S-trap is illegal because it's siphon-prone. This is a code violation under IPC/UPC. So decision should be "fail". Risk: since it can cause trap seal loss and sewer gas entry, it's a health hazard, so risk likely "high" or "critical". Probably "high" rather than critical because it's roughed in but not in service, but I'd lean "high". Work stage: "rough-complete". Component conditions: "installed-defective" and "non-compliant" (since it's installed but defective and doesn't comply). Percent complete: likely around 80%? But the S-trap is a major flaw, so the trap/venting work isn't complete correctly. I'll say 70%? Need to estimate overall progress of the trap/venting scope. It's roughed in, so installed but not closed out. Maybe 75%? I'll set 70. Value: not applicable. Sound source: not needed. Confidence: high, 0.95. Event time, rate, order, workable: not needed. Findings: ["S-trap configuration installed, which is prohibited due to siphonage potential"]. Actions: ["Replace S-trap with a proper P-trap and ensure adequate venting per code"]. Rationale: "S-traps are not allowed by plumbing codes because they are prone to siphoning the trap seal, allowing sewer gas entry. A proper trap and venting arrangement must be installed." References: ["IPC 1002.1", "UPC 1002.1"] (specific sections on traps). Actually, P-traps are required, and venting requirements. So I'll quote IPC 1002.1 and UPC 1002.1 or similar. I'll put "IPC 1002.1 (Traps required), UPC 1002.1" etc. Might also note IPC 909.1? Keep it simple.

Now output JSON.

events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "rough-complete",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 70,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.95,
  "event_time": 0.0,
  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],

  "findings": ["S-trap configuration installed, which is prohibited due to siphonage potential"],
  "actions": ["Replace S-trap with a proper P-trap and ensure adequate venting per code"],
  "rationale": "S-traps are not allowed by plumbing codes because they are prone to siphoning the trap seal, allowing sewer gas entry.",
  "references": ["IPC 1002.1", "UPC 1002.1"]
}
